title: End-to-End Test Definition
description: Defines a single end-to-end test for the Hardhat test harness.

description.description: Short human-readable summary of what this scenario tests

repo.description: GitHub repo in org/name format (e.g. OpenZeppelin/openzeppelin-contracts)

commit.description: Full SHA or tag to checkout

packageManager.description: Package manager used by the repo

defaultCommand.description: Default command to run when --command is not provided to exec

preinstall.description: Relative path to a shell script for patching the repo before install

install.description: Relative path to a custom install script (overrides built-in Verdaccio flow)

env.description: Environment variables to set during install/exec. Values may reference host variables with the devcontainer-style token ${localEnv:NAME} (e.g. "FOUNDRY_RPC_URL": "${localEnv:ALCHEMY_URL}"); the referenced host variable must be set or the run will fail.

disabled.description: Set to true to skip this scenario during end-to-end runs

skip.description: Set to true to skip this scenario in the regression benchmark

runs.description: Number of hyperfine runs for this command

prepare.description: Optional shell snippet to run before each timed run (passed to hyperfine --prepare)

commands.description: Named commands to benchmark. Iterated in definition order; the key becomes the benchmark name on disk.

benchmark.description: Benchmark configuration for pnpm bench and pnpm bench:regression
